evals.prompt: Define an availability SLO for our checkout service. It's revenue-critical. Define the SLI, set a target, and calculate the monthly error budget.
expectations: Defines the SLI clearly (e.g., successful HTTP 2xx/3xx responses / total valid requests)
Sets a highly available target appropriate for revenue (e.g., 99.9% or 99.95%)
Calculates the exact error budget in minutes (e.g., 43.2 minutes/month for 99.9%)
Excludes invalid requests (e.g., 4xx errors caused by user input) from the SLI calculation

evals.prompt: Write an error budget policy. What happens when the team burns 100% of their error budget?
evals.expected_output: An error budget policy defining consequences and actions.
expectations: Clearly states the consequence of exhausting the budget (e.g., feature freeze, no non-emergency deploys)
Mandates shifting engineering focus to reliability work
Includes a process for reviewing the budget and reinstating feature work
